root_cause.text: The application evaluates user-controlled input (such as OAuth scopes or error messages) as Spring Expression Language (SpEL) expressions via the `SpelView` class, leading to Server-Side Template Injection (SSTI) and potential Remote Code Execution (RCE).
root_cause.evidence: The patch removes `SpelView.java`, which executed expressions using `parser.parseExpression(name).getValue(context)`, and adds a test `testApprovalPageWithSuspectScope` that verifies the malicious SpEL payload `${T(java.lang.Runtime).getRuntime().exec("cd ..")}` is escaped using `HtmlUtils.htmlEscape()` instead of being executed.

agent_logs.root_cause_candidate: The application evaluates user-controlled input (such as OAuth scopes or error messages) as Spring Expression Language (SpEL) expressions via the `SpelView` class, leading to Server-Side Template Injection (SSTI) and potential Remote Code Execution (RCE).
agent_logs.evidence_points: The patch removes `SpelView.java`, which executed expressions using `parser.parseExpression(name).getValue(context)`, and adds a test `testApprovalPageWithSuspectScope` that verifies the malicious SpEL payload `${T(java.lang.Runtime).getRuntime().exec("cd ..")}` is escaped using `HtmlUtils.htmlEscape()` instead of being executed.

intent_record.intent_summary: Remove the SpelView class to eliminate its template rendering functionality.
intent_record.how: Removes the SpelView class, which was used for rendering templates using Spring Expression Language (SpEL).

intent_record.intent_summary: Update and reorganize imports to support new dependencies.
intent_record.what: Reordered existing imports and added new ones including AuthorizationRequest, CsrfToken, View, ServletUriComponentsBuilder, HtmlUtils, and HttpServletResponse.
intent_record.how: Makes the newly imported classes available for use within the file, likely to support new CSRF token handling and view rendering logic.

intent_record.intent_summary: Replace SpEL-based view rendering with explicit programmatic HTML construction and HTML escaping to prevent template injection and XSS vulnerabilities.
intent_record.what: Removed the use of SpelView and static HTML string templates replacing '%vars%', in favor of generating HTML programmatically using StringBuilder and dynamically evaluating variables directly in code. Also added HTML escaping to dynamic content like clientId, csrfToken parameters, and scope.
intent_record.how: Eliminates Spring Expression Language (SpEL) evaluation from the view rendering process, mitigating potential Server-Side Template Injection (SSTI) or Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) vulnerabilities. HTML content is explicitly built and properly escaped (using HtmlUtils.htmlEscape) before being written directly to the response.

intent_record.what: Reorganized existing imports and added new imports for View and HttpServletResponse.
intent_record.how: This change has no impact on runtime behavior as it only updates the Java import statements.

intent_record.intent_summary: Change error template placeholder syntax to prevent unintended expression evaluation.
intent_record.what: Changed the placeholder syntax in the ERROR template string from a dollar sign notation to percentage signs.
intent_record.how: Prevents the framework from unintentionally evaluating the error summary as an executable expression.

intent_record.intent_summary: Fix potential SpEL injection vulnerability by removing SpelView from the whitelabel error page.
intent_record.what: Replaced the SpelView rendering with a custom View that uses basic string replacement.
intent_record.how: Prevents the error summary from being evaluated as a Spring Expression Language (SpEL) expression, avoiding unintended code execution.

intent_record.what: The entire SpelViewTests class and its corresponding file were deleted.
intent_record.how: It removes the unit tests for the SpelView class from the test suite.

intent_record.intent_summary: Remove unused imports to clean up the code.
intent_record.what: Removed unused import statements for JUnit Assert and Java util collections.
intent_record.how: Does not alter runtime behavior, but cleans up the compilation unit by removing unnecessary dependencies.

intent_record.intent_summary: Add necessary imports for collections and assertions.
intent_record.what: Added Java util and JUnit assert imports.
intent_record.how: Makes these classes available for use in the test class.

intent_record.intent_summary: Add tests to verify correct HTML generation and escaping of malicious scopes in the Whitelabel Approval Endpoint.
intent_record.what: Added three new unit tests (testApprovalPageWithSuspectScope, testApprovalPageWithScopesInForm, testApprovalPageWithoutScopesInForm) to WhitelabelApprovalEndpointTests.
intent_record.how: Verifies that the endpoint properly escapes potentially malicious scopes (SPEL injection/XSS) and generates the expected HTML form content both with and without scopes present.

intent_record.intent_summary: Update authorization page test assertions to expect double quotes instead of single quotes in HTML attributes.
intent_record.what: Changed single quotes to double quotes in string assertions for HTML attributes.
intent_record.how: Updates the test to match the new HTML output format, preventing test failures.

intent_record.intent_summary: Update test assertions to expect double quotes instead of single quotes for HTML attributes.
intent_record.what: Replaced single quotes with double quotes in HTML attribute string literals within test assertions.
intent_record.how: Updates the test expectations to match the actual HTML output, which now uses double quotes for attributes, ensuring the tests pass.

intent_record.intent_summary: Update test assertions to match HTML output using double quotes instead of single quotes.
intent_record.what: Changed single quotes to double quotes in HTML attribute strings used in test assertions.
intent_record.how: Updates the test assertions to correctly match the updated HTML rendering output which now uses double quotes, preventing test failures.

intent_record.intent_summary: Update test assertions to match HTML output using double quotes.
intent_record.what: Changed single quotes to double quotes in string assertions for HTML attributes.
intent_record.how: Updates the test to correctly match the generated HTML output which now uses double quotes.

grouping_result.intent: Add necessary imports for collections and assertions.
Fix potential SpEL injection vulnerability by removing SpelView from the whitelabel error page.
Remove unused imports to clean up the code.
Add tests to verify correct HTML generation and escaping of malicious scopes in the Whitelabel Approval Endpoint.
Update test assertions to match HTML output using double quotes.

consistency_review.core_intent: Remove the SpelView class and its associated test cases
Update and add necessary imports for collections, assertions, and new dependencies.
Fix SpEL/template injection vulnerabilities in the whitelabel error page by replacing SpelView with explicit HTML construction and escaping.
Clean up, reorganize, and update import statements.
Update test assertions to match HTML output by expecting double quotes instead of single quotes in HTML attributes.

relevance_review.reason: The group removes the SpelView class, which is explicitly identified as the root cause of the SpEL evaluation vulnerability, directly addressing the issue.
The group only contains updates and additions to import statements, which are structural changes and do not directly address the SpEL evaluation vulnerability.
The group directly addresses the vulnerability by removing the vulnerable SpelView class and replacing it with explicit HTML construction and escaping, preventing the SpEL injection.
The group only contains changes to reorganize and clean up import statements, which is a refactoring activity unrelated to the SpEL evaluation vulnerability.
The group adds tests to verify the proper escaping of malicious input (OAuth scopes) in the endpoint affected by the vulnerability, directly validating the fix for the root cause.
The group only updates test assertions for HTML attribute quotes, which is an unrelated test fix and does not directly address the SpEL injection vulnerability.

candidates.rationale: This line constructs a SpelView with a template that incorporates user-controlled inputs (scopes), allowing SpEL injection/SSTI.
This line constructs a SpelView with an error template containing user-controlled error messages, resulting in SpEL injection/SSTI.

vuln_statements.explanation: This statement uses SpelView to evaluate a template containing user-controlled scopes, leading to Server-Side Template Injection (SSTI).
This statement uses SpelView to evaluate an error template containing user-controlled error messages, leading to Server-Side Template Injection (SSTI).

If user defines a @Bean of type ApprovalStore the Authoriza
bic_trace_step.code_snippet:
commit 787c9b47d801b0cf08d35bca4805986c43f56836
Author: Dave Syer <dsyer@gopivotal.com>
Date:   Mon Mar 17 16:14:03 2014 +0000

    Add support for ApprovalStore in @EnableAuthorizationServer
    
    If user defines a @Bean of type ApprovalStore the AuthorizationEndpoint and
    WhitelabelApprovalEndpoint change their behaviour accordingly.

diff --git a/spring-security-oauth2/src/main/java/org/springframework/security/oauth2/provider/endpoint/WhitelabelApprovalEndpoint.java b/spring-security-o
bic_trace_step.reason: The function `getAccessConfirmation` passes a dynamically created template (via `createTemplate`) to `SpelView`. The `createScopes` method embeds user-controlled scope strings directly into the template (using `.replace("%scope%", scope)`), which are then evaluated as SpEL expressions by `SpelView`, leading to the SSTI vulnerability. The fix is not present.

diff --git a/spring-security-oauth2/src/main/java/org/springframework/security/oauth2/

The commit modifies the method signature and model binding but does not fix the underlying SpEL evaluation vulnerability.

bic_trace_step.reason: This commit introduces the `SpelView` class which evaluates user input via `parser.parseExpression(name)` inside the `PlaceholderResolver`. This allows Server-Side Template Injection (SSTI) via SpEL expressions. There is no sanitization or safe evaluation context applied to prevent RCE.

If user defines a @Bean of type ApprovalStore the Authoriza
bic_trace_step.code_snippet:
commit 787c9b47d801b0cf08d35bca4805986c43f56836
Author: Dave Syer <dsyer@gopivotal.com>
Date:   Mon Mar 17 16:14:03 2014 +0000

    Add support for ApprovalStore in @EnableAuthorizationServer
    
    If user defines a @Bean of type ApprovalStore the AuthorizationEndpoint and
    WhitelabelApprovalEndpoint change their behaviour accordingly.

diff --git a/spring-security-oauth2/src/main/java/org/springframework/security/oauth2/provider/endpoint/WhitelabelErrorEndpoint.java b/spring-security-oaut
bic_trace_step.reason: The commit introduces `WhitelabelErrorEndpoint` which passes user-controlled error data to a `SpelView`. The `ERROR` template contains `${error.summary}`, leading to SpEL evaluation of the error object, which is the root cause of the SSTI vulnerability. No sanitization or safe evaluation logic is present.
